ai_enrichment.summary: The native Windows utility systeminfo.exe was executed to enumerate detailed system configuration including OS version, hardware specs, hotfixes, and network configuration. This execution was spawned by a PowerShell script explicitly located in the Atomic Red Team testing framework directory, strongly indicating this is part of an authorized adversary simulation exercise rather than genuine threat actor activity.
ioc_indicators: ParentImage: powershell.exe with Atomic Red Team script
investigation_steps: 1. Confirm with security team whether Atomic Red Team testing is currently authorized on Win10-Victim (192.168.56.102)
2. Verify the labuser account is authorized to perform security testing activities
3. Review process tree for additional discovery commands executed before/after systeminfo (whoami, net user, ipconfig, etc.)
4. Check for data exfiltration attempts - did output get written to file or sent over network
5. Query for other T1082 test artifacts: Get-ComputerInfo, hostname, wmic os get commands
6. Review C:\AtomicRedTeam directory for complete list of tests executed during this session
containment_actions: No immediate containment required if Atomic Red Team testing is authorized
If unauthorized: Isolate endpoint from network pending investigation
If unauthorized: Disable labuser account and revoke active sessions
Document test execution for purple team exercise records
ai_enrichment.false_positive_reasoning: The parent process explicitly references C:\AtomicRedTeam\atomics\T1082\T1082.ps1, which is the standard Atomic Red Team test case for MITRE T1082, strongly indicating an authorized security test rather than malicious reconnaissance.
